description_long: This apartment building is a tall, rectangular high-rise structure with a beige and brown color scheme, featuring a grid of evenly spaced windows across its facade. The building is divided into two main vertical sections, separated by a horizontal band, and topped with a dark, mechanical-looking rooftop structure. The lower and upper sections both display a consistent window pattern, with some windows highlighted by darker frames or shading. Balconies are visible on the darker sides, adding depth and texture to the exterior. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation, and the building appears to be constructed primarily from concrete and glass, typical of urban residential towers.
description: A tall, beige and brown high-rise apartment building with a grid of windows and balconies, divided into two main sections and topped with a dark rooftop structure.
description_view_0: The beige facade with a grid of windows is prominent, and the horizontal band dividing the two main sections is clearly visible.
description_view_1: This side features more visible balconies and a darker color scheme, with illuminated windows suggesting interior lighting.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, this side shows the beige facade and window grid, but with subtle differences in window shading and fewer visible balconies.
description_view_3: This view highlights the balconies and darker facade, with the window grid and horizontal band still visible but less pronounced.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a regular, symmetrical grid pattern, evenly spaced both vertically and horizontally across all visible facades.
